title: Neural Network Architectures
description: A bottom-up tour of the core neural network architectures — how they work, why they are designed the way they are, and when to use each. Covers MLPs, convolutional layers and ResNets, vanilla RNNs, LSTMs and GRUs, scaled dot-product and multi-head attention, and the transformer (encoder-only, decoder-only, encoder-decoder). Builds the architectural vocabulary assumed by every other supplement.

readings.title: The MLP — Layers, Activations, and Universal Approximation
readings.description: Affine + non-linearity, the MLP forward pass, Universal Approximation Theorem, depth vs. width, layer normalization, and dropout.

readings.title: Convolutional Layers and the Vision Inductive Bias
readings.description: Convolution operation, weight sharing, translation equivariance, kernel size / stride / padding, receptive fields, pooling variants, and depthwise separable convolutions.

readings.title: Going Deeper — ResNets and Residual Connections
readings.description: The degradation problem, residual blocks (F(x)+x), the gradient highway, basic vs. bottleneck blocks, projection shortcuts, and residual connections in transformers.

readings.title: Vanilla RNNs and the Vanishing Gradient
readings.description: Recurrent forward pass, weight sharing across time, BPTT, the vanishing and exploding gradient problems, gradient clipping, and RNN configurations (many-to-one, seq2seq).

readings.title: LSTM and GRU — Gating Solutions to Long-Range Memory
readings.description: LSTM's four gates, cell state as a gradient highway, GRU's reset and update gates, LSTM vs. GRU parameter count and performance comparison.

readings.title: Attention Mechanisms
readings.description: Query-key-value abstraction, scaled dot-product attention, the √d_k scaling, multi-head attention, self-attention vs. masked self-attention vs. cross-attention.

readings.title: The Transformer
readings.description: Encoder and decoder block structure, FFN sub-layer, positional encodings (sinusoidal, learned, RoPE), encoder-only / decoder-only / encoder-decoder families, and efficiency improvements (GQA, FlashAttention).
